output.final_deliverable:
1. Capital city of Australia: Canberra

Cited authoritative source (from the merged results):
1. https://www.britannica.com/place/Australia

Note: The merged results included the above authoritative URL (Britannica). The merged results did not include an extracted page snippet explicitly showing the word "Canberra," so the citation is the URL listed in the provided results.
output.action_summary: Answered: Canberra is the capital city of Australia. Cited one authoritative URL from the merged results: https://www.britannica.com/place/Australia. The merged results did not contain an explicit page excerpt proving the capital name, so the answer is cited to the listed Britannica URL.

result.query: capital city of Australia authoritative source official

vector_context: Synthesize final answer and format concise response with one citation

results.title: Australia | History, Cities, Population, Capital, Map, & Facts | Britannica
results.description: There are also two internal territories: Northern Territory, established as a self-governing territory in 1978, and the Australian Capital Territory (including the city of Canberra), which attained self-governing status in 1988. The federal authorities govern the external territories of Norfolk Island, the Cocos (Keeling) Islands, Christmas Island, Ashmore and Cartier islands, the Coral Sea Islands, and Heard Island and McDonald Islands and claim the Australian Antarctic Territory, an area larger than Australia itself.

results.title: List of Australian capital cities - Wikipedia
results.description: There are eight capital cities in Australia, each of which functions as the seat of government for the state or territory in which it is located. One of these, Canberra, is also the national capital. Section 125 of the Constitution of Australia ...

results.title: Canberra - Wikipedia
results.description: The Commonwealth government purchased the pastoral property of Yarralumla in 1913 to provide an official residence for the Governor-General of Australia in the new capital. Renovations began in 1925 to enlarge and modernise the property. In 1927, the property was officially dubbed Government House. On 9 May that year, the Commonwealth parliament moved to Canberra with the opening of the Provisional Parliament House. The Prime Minister Stanley Bruce had officially taken up residence in The Lodge a few days earlier. Planned development of the city slowed significantly during the depression of the 1930s and during World War II.

results.title: Canberra | History, Map, Population, Climate, & Facts | Britannica
results.description: Construction began in 1913 but was interrupted by World War I. On May 9, 1927, ceremonies marked the official transfer of the federal Parliament from Melbourne to the new capital. Canberra: Legislative Assembly buildingLegislative Assembly building for the Australian Capital Territory, Canberra, Australia.(more) Canberra lies on a plain at the foot of 6,200-foot (1,900-metre) spurs of the Australian Alps, enjoying warm summers and cool winters and receiving considerably less rainfall than the surrounding highlands. The city has been expanding, and its population has grown steadily.

results.title: Australian states, territories and capital cities - Tourism Australia
results.description: Victoria (VIC) is the thriving heart of all things culture in Australia, centred in the state’s capital, Melbourne. The city has been the recipient of the ‘Most Liveable City in the World’ title multiple times, owing to its cultural diversity, ...

details.intent: Retrieve the full page content and extract the explicit statement of the capital city, plus a short quoted snippet to use as a citation.
target_facts: evidence_snippet (exact sentence/phrase from the page mentioning the capital)
extraction_plan: Locate the page section or infobox that labels 'Capital' or the phrase 'Capital:'.
Extract the exact word(s) naming the capital and capture the surrounding sentence as an evidence_snippet (<= 300 characters).
Return capital_name, evidence_snippet, and citation_url exactly as on the page.
verification_steps: Confirm the extracted capital_name appears in the page text and matches common knowledge (e.g., 'Canberra').
If the CIA page does not explicitly name the capital in clear text, fall back to the top search result that is a government or Britannica page (these URLs would come from the search node).
